message.content:
Explore the test setup in /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service. I need to understand:
1. Jest configuration files (jest.config.ts, jest.coverage.config.ts, any in package.json)
2. tsconfig.test.json contents
3. A few example unit test files from src/unit-tests/ (pick 2-3 representative ones)
4. A few example e2e test files from test/ (pick 2-3 representative ones including the env-config one)
5. Any test helpers or setup files (jest.setup.ts, test factories, mocks)
6. How coverage thresholds are configured
7. How e2e tests bootstrap the NestJS app (test module setup pattern)

Return the full file contents for all config files and representative test examples.

1→{
     2→	"name": "recommendations-service",
     3→	"version": "0.0.1",
     4→	"description": "recommendations service for CI",
     5→	"author": "4thwithme",
     6→	"private": true,
     7→	"engines": {
     8→		"node": ">=v24.13.0",
     9→		"npm": ">=11.0.0"
    10→	},
    11→	"license": "UNLICENSED",
    12→	"scripts": {
    13→		"build": "nest build && npm run build:migrations && npm run copy:assets",
    14→		"build:migrations": "tsc --project src/migrations/tsconfig.json && tsc-alias -p src/migrations/tsconfig.json",
    15→		"copy:assets": "cp -r src/assets dist/assets && mkdir -p dist/seeds && cp src/seeds/*.csv dist/seeds/",
    16→		"format": "prettier --write \"src/**/*.ts\" \"test/**/*.ts\"",
    17→		"type-check": "tsc --noEmit",
    18→		"start": "nest start -e 'node -r newrelic'",
    19→		"start:dev": "nest start --watch --webpack --webpackPath webpack.config.js",
    20→		"🐎": "nest start --watch --webpack --webpackPath webpack.config.js",
    21→		"start:debug": "nest start --debug --watch --webpack --webpackPath webpack.config.js",
    22→		"start:prod": "node dist/main",
    23→		"lint": "eslint \"{src,apps,libs,test}/**/*.ts\"",
    24→		"lint:fix": "eslint \"{src,apps,libs,test}/**/*.ts\" --fix",
    25→		"code-quality-check": "npm run lint && npm run format && npm run type-check",
    26→		"test:unit": "dotenv -e .env.test -- npx jest --config ./test/jest-unit.config.ts",
    27→		"test:e2e": "npm run db:reset:test && dotenv -e .env.test -- npx jest --runInBand --forceExit --config ./test/jest-e2e.config.ts",
    28→		"test:unit:coverage": "dotenv -e .env.test -- npx jest --config ./test/jest-unit.coverage.config.ts",
    29→		"test:e2e:coverage": "npm run db:reset:test && dotenv -e .env.test -- npx jest --runInBand --forceExit --config ./test/jest-e2e.coverage.config.ts",
    30→		"test": "npm run code-quality-check && (npm run test:unit & npm run test:e2e & wait)",
    31→		"test:coverage": "npm run code-quality-check && (npm run test:unit:coverage & npm run test:e2e:coverage & wait)",
    32→		"prepare": "husky || true",
    33→		"artillery-load-test": "npx artillery run --environment test --output report.json --record --key $ARTILLERY_KEY ./src/scripts/load-testing/artillery.yml",
    34→		"cli:dev": "ts-node --log-error -r tsconfig-paths/register src/cli.ts",
    35→		"cli:prod": "node dist/cli.js",
    36→		"migrate": "ts-node -r dotenv/config -r tsconfig-paths/register ./node_modules/.bin/knex migrate:latest --knexfile ./src/knexfile.ts && npm run generate:db-uml",
    37→		"migrate:prod": "node -r dotenv/config ./node_modules/.bin/knex migrate:latest --knexfile ./dist/knexfile.prod.js",
    38→		"migrate:rollback": "ts-node -r dotenv/config -r tsconfig-paths/register ./node_modules/.bin/knex migrate:rollback --knexfile ./src/knexfile.ts",
    39→		"migrate:up": "ts-node -r dotenv/config -r tsconfig-paths/register ./node_modules/.bin/knex migrate:up --knexfile ./src/knexfile.ts && npm run generate:db-uml",
    40→		"migrate:down": "ts-node -r dotenv/config -r tsconfig-paths/register ./node_modules/.bin/knex migrate:down --knexfile ./src/knexfile.ts",
    41→		"migrate:make": "ts-node -r dotenv/config -r tsconfig-paths/register ./node_modules/.bin/knex migrate:make --knexfile ./src/knexfile.ts -x ts",
    42→		"db:reset:test": "dotenv -e .env.test -- ts-node -r tsconfig-paths/register ./scripts/reset-test-db.ts",
    43→		"generate:db-uml": "dotenv -e .env -- ts-node -r tsconfig-paths/register ./src/scripts/generate-db-uml.script.ts"

1→import { Knex } from 'knex';
     2→

     9→
    10→export const TEST_UUID = '123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000';
    11→
    12→export function createMockRelatedProduct(
    13→	overrides: Partial<IRelatedProduct> = {},
    14→): IRelatedProduct {
    15→	return {
    16→		style_id: 100,
    17→		color: 1,
    18→		link: '/product/100',
    19→		sizing: 'S - XL',
    20→		sizes: ['S', 'M', 'L', 'XL'],
    21→		min_qty: 12,
    22→		name: 'Test Product',
    23→		image_src: 'https://example.com/100.jpg',
    24→		rating_score: '4.5',
    25→		rating_count: '50',
    26→		style_type: 'apparel',
    27→		brand: ['TestBrand'],
    28→		default_quote_qty: 25,
    29→		primary_category_id: 10,
    30→		primary_category_name: 'T-Shirts',
    31→		broad_category_names: ['Apparel'],
    32→		sub_category_names: ['T-Shirts'],
    33→		default_unit_prices: [{ algorithm: 'default', quantity: 12, price: '10.99' }],
    34→		...overrides,

    36→}
    37→
    38→export function createMockStyle(overrides: Partial<IStyleBase> = {}): IStyleBase {
    39→	return {
    40→		_id: '1',
    41→		style_id: 123456,
    42→		primary_category_id: 10,
    43→		root_category_id: 10,
    44→		purchases_count: 100,
    45→		categories_ids: [10, 15],
    46→		categories_with_parents_ids: [5, 10, 15],
    47→		is_active: true,
    48→		name: 'Style Name',
    49→		decoration_method: 'Decoration Method',
    50→		features: 'Features',
    51→		general_comments: 'General Comments',
    52→		manufacturer: 'Manufacturer',
    53→		minimum_quantity: 1,
    54→		default_quote_qty: 1,
    55→		min_qty: 1,
    56→		rating_count: 1,
    57→		rating_score: 1,
    58→		sizes: ['Size 1', 'Size 2'],
    59→		style_type: 'Style Type',
    60→		price_level: 1,
    61→		product_type: 'Product Type',
    62→		primary_category_name: 'Primary Category Name',
    63→		broad_category_names: ['Broad Category 1', 'Broad Category 2'],
    64→		sub_category_names: ['Sub Category 1', 'Sub Category 2'],
    65→		style_card_image_url: 'https://example.com/style-card.png',
    66→		link_path: 'https://example.com/style-link',
    67→		brand: ['Brand 1', 'Brand 2'],
    68→		turn_time: 1,
    69→		rush_turn_time: 1,
    70→		no_minimum: true,
    71→		created_at: new Date('2024-01-01'),
    72→		updated_at: new Date('2024-01-01'),
    73→		deleted_at: null,
    74→		...overrides,
    75→	};
    76→}
    77→
    78→export function createMockStyleColor(
    79→	overrides: Partial<IStyleColorBase> = {},
    80→): IStyleColorBase {
    81→	return {
    82→		_id: '1',
    83→		style_id: 123456,
    84→		color_id: 1,
    85→		color_rgb: '#FF0000',
    86→		color_name: 'Red',
    87→		image_url: 'https://example.com/red.png',
    88→		is_active: true,
    89→		created_at: new Date('2024-01-01'),
    90→		updated_at: new Date('2024-01-01'),
    91→		deleted_at: null,
    92→		...overrides,
    93→	};
    94→}
    95→
    96→export async function createMockStyleInDB(
    97→	knex: Knex,
    98→	styleData: Partial<IStyleBase> = {},
    99→): Promise<void> {
   100→	const uniqueId = styleData._id ?? `style_${styleData.style_id ?? Date.now()}`;
   101→	const mockStyle = createMockStyle({ ...styleData, _id: uniqueId });
   102→
   103→	const existing = await knex(TABLE_NAME_STYLES)
   104→		.where({ style_id: mockStyle.style_id })
   105→		.first();
   106→
   107→	if (!existing) {
   108→		await knex(TABLE_NAME_STYLES).insert({
   109→			...mockStyle,
   110→			categories_ids: JSON.stringify(mockStyle.categories_ids),
   111→			categories_with_parents_ids: JSON.stringify(mockStyle.categories_with_parents_ids),
   112→			sizes: JSON.stringify(mockStyle.sizes),
   113→			broad_category_names: JSON.stringify(mockStyle.broad_category_names),
   114→			sub_category_names: JSON.stringify(mockStyle.sub_category_names),
   115→			brand: JSON.stringify(mockStyle.brand),
   116→		});
   117→	}
   118→}
   119→

content.text: Let me check for more e2e test examples and look at a test CLAUDE.md file if it exists:

content.content:
1→# E2E Tests
     2→
     3→## Location & Naming
     4→
     5→- Files: `*.e2e-spec.ts` in `test/`
     6→- Run: `npm run test:e2e`
     7→- Coverage: `npm run test:e2e:coverage`
     8→- DB reset: `npm run db:reset:test` (runs automatically via `test:e2e`)
     9→
    10→## Scope
    11→
    12→Test these:
    13→- REST endpoints via real HTTP requests (supertest)
    14→- Full module integration (controller → service → DB)
    15→- Database read/write operations
    16→- Cache behavior
    17→- Controller method dispatch (without HTTP server)
    18→- Service logic with mocked dependencies
    19→
    20→Do NOT e2e test:
    21→- Pure utility logic (covered by unit tests)
    22→- Internal method logic (covered by unit tests)
    23→
    24→## App Bootstrap Pattern (HTTP Tests)
    25→
    26→```typescript
    27→import { INestApplication } from '@nestjs/common';
    28→import { Test, TestingModule } from '@nestjs/testing';
    29→import * as request from 'supertest';
    30→import { App } from 'supertest/types';
    31→
    32→import { CacheModule } from '@modules/cache/cache.module';
    33→import { EnvConfigModule } from '@modules/env-config/env-config.module';
    34→import { CorrelationIdMiddleware } from '../src/middlewares/correlation-id.middleware';
    35→
    36→describe('FeatureName API Integration Tests', () => {
    37→  let app: INestApplication;
    38→  let getHttpServer: App;
    39→
    40→  beforeAll(async () => {
    41→    const moduleFixture: TestingModule = await Test.createTestingModule({
    42→      imports: [EnvConfigModule, CacheModule],
    43→      controllers: [FeatureController],
    44→      providers: [
    45→        FeatureService,
    46→        {
    47→          provide: ExternalService,
    48→          useValue: { method: jest.fn() },
    49→        },
    50→      ],
    51→    }).compile();
    52→
    53→    app = moduleFixture.createNestApplication();
    54→
    55→    const correlationIdMiddleware = new CorrelationIdMiddleware();
    56→    app.use(correlationIdMiddleware.use.bind(correlationIdMiddleware));
    57→
    58→    getHttpServer = app.getHttpServer() as App;
    59→
    60→    await app.init();
    61→  });
    62→
    63→  afterAll(async () => {
    64→    await app.close();
    65→  });
    66→});
    67→```
    68→
    69→## Making HTTP Requests
    70→
    71→```typescript
    72→const response = await request(getHttpServer)
    73→  .get('/api/v1/endpoint')
    74→  .query({ param: 'value' });
    75→
    76→expect(response.status).toBe(200);
    77→expect(response.body).toMatchObject({ expected: 'shape' });
    78→```
    79→
    80→## Controller Tests (Without HTTP Server)
    81→
    82→Some e2e tests test controllers directly without spinning up an HTTP server:
    83→
    84→```typescript
    85→describe('FeatureController', () => {
    86→  let controller: FeatureController;
    87→  let mockService: jest.Mocked<FeatureService>;
    88→
    89→  beforeEach(async () => {
    90→    mockService = {
    91→      getData: jest.fn(),
    92→    } as unknown as jest.Mocked<FeatureService>;
    93→
    94→    const module: TestingModule = await Test.createTestingModule({
    95→      controllers: [FeatureController],
    96→      providers: [{ provide: FeatureService, useValue: mockService }],
    97→    }).compile();
    98→
    99→    controller = module.get<FeatureController>(FeatureController);
   100→  });
   101→
   102→  afterEach(() => {
   103→    jest.clearAllMocks();
   104→  });
   105→});
   106→```
   107→
   108→## Mocking Strategy
   109→
   110→Two approaches used in this codebase:
   111→
   112→1. **Inline mock providers** (most common): Pass `{ provide: X, useValue: mockObj }` in the `providers` array.
   113→2. **`.overrideProvider()`**: Chain on `Test.createTestingModule({...}).overrideProvider(X).useValue(mockObj).compile()`.
   114→
   115→Mock all external services (Algolia, MMS, OpenAI, AWS). Use real DB (test DB is reset before suite). Use real `CacheModule` for cache integration tests; spy on `CacheService` methods as needed.
   116→
   117→Apply `CorrelationIdMiddleware` manually after `createNestApplication()` for HTTP tests. Add `ValidationPipe` when the endpoint uses DTO validation.
   118→
   119→## Coverage Requirements
   120→
   121→95% minimum across branches, functions, lines, and statements.
   122→Coverage is collected only from `*.(controller|service).ts` files.
   123→Configured in `test/jest-e2e.coverage.config.ts` (extends `test/jest.coverage.config.ts`).
   124→Run `npm run test:e2e:coverage` to verify before committing.
   125→
   126→Excluded from coverage:
   127→- `**/load-test/**`
   128→- `src/modules/newrelic/newrelic.service.ts`
   129→- `src/modules/rest/related-products/algolia.service.ts`
   130→- `src/modules/rest/related-products/mms.service.ts`
   131→- `src/modules/bigquery/**`
   132→- `src/modules/cli/**`
   133→- `src/modules/db/style-copurchases/style-copurchases.service.ts`
   134→- `src/modules/color-match-worker/color-match-worker.service.ts`
   135→
   136→## Database
   137→
   138→Test DB is MySQL replica. Reset runs automatically before e2e suite.
   139→For manual reset: `npm run db:reset:test`.
   140→Never use production DB for tests.
   141→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1→# Unit Tests
     2→
     3→## Location & Naming
     4→
     5→- Files: `*.unit-spec.ts` in `src/unit-tests/`
     6→- Run: `npm run test:unit`
     7→- Coverage: `npm run test:unit:coverage`
     8→
     9→## Scope
    10→
    11→Test these:
    12→- Utility functions (`src/utils/`)
    13→- Service layer methods (`src/modules/**/*.service.ts`)
    14→- Decorators (`src/decorators/`)
    15→- Model methods (`src/modules/db/**/models/*.model.ts`)
    16→- Config files (`src/configs/`)
    17→
    18→Do NOT unit test:
    19→- Controllers (covered by e2e tests)
    20→- Full module integration (covered by e2e tests)
    21→- Third-party integrations (mock at boundary)
    22→
    23→## Mocking Pattern
    24→
    25→Always use `Test.createTestingModule` and provide mocks via `useValue`. Never instantiate real services.
    26→
    27→```typescript
    28→import { Test, TestingModule } from '@nestjs/testing';
    29→
    30→const module: TestingModule = await Test.createTestingModule({
    31→  providers: [
    32→    ServiceUnderTest,
    33→    {
    34→      provide: DependencyService,
    35→      useValue: { methodName: jest.fn() },
    36→    },
    37→    {
    38→      provide: KNEX_READ_CONNECTION,
    39→      useValue: mockKnexRead,
    40→    },
    41→    {
    42→      provide: KNEX_WRITE_CONNECTION,
    43→      useValue: mockKnexWrite,
    44→    },
    45→  ],
    46→}).compile();
    47→```
    48→
    49→Use `jest.Mocked<T>` for typed mocks:
    50→
    51→```typescript
    52→let service: jest.Mocked<DependencyService>;
    53→service = { methodName: jest.fn() } as jest.Mocked<DependencyService>;
    54→```
    55→
    56→Always `jest.clearAllMocks()` in `afterEach`.
    57→
    58→## Test Structure
    59→
    60→```typescript
    61→describe('ClassName', () => {
    62→  let subject: SubjectClass;
    63→  let mockDep: jest.Mocked<DepClass>;
    64→
    65→  beforeEach(async () => {
    66→    // set up mocks and module
    67→  });
    68→
    69→  afterEach(() => {
    70→    jest.clearAllMocks();
    71→  });
    72→
    73→  describe('methodName', () => {
    74→    it('should [expected behavior] when [condition]', async () => {
    75→      // arrange
    76→      // act
    77→      // assert
    78→    });
    79→  });
    80→});
    81→```
    82→
    83→Test every meaningful branch: happy path, error path, edge cases (null, empty, boundary values).
    84→
    85→## Coverage Requirements (STRICT — NON-NEGOTIABLE)
    86→
    87→**95% minimum across ALL metrics: branches, functions, lines, statements.**
    88→
    89→This threshold is enforced by Jest and will fail CI if not met. Every new test file you write must meet this bar. When writing tests:
    90→
    91→- Cover every branch: `if/else`, ternaries, optional chaining fallbacks, early returns
    92→- Cover every error path: thrown exceptions, rejected promises, caught errors
    93→- Cover edge cases: `null`, `undefined`, empty arrays/strings, boundary numeric values
    94→- Do not write tests that trivially pass without asserting meaningful behavior
    95→
    96→`/modules/db/` patterns are excluded from unit coverage config, but all other code must meet 95%.
    97→
    98→Run `npm run test:unit:coverage` to verify before committing. Never commit tests that cause coverage to drop below 95%.
    99→
   100→## Proactive Coverage Improvement
   101→
   102→When working on tests, **always check overall coverage first**:
   103→
   104→```bash
   105→npm run test:unit:coverage
   106→```
   107→
   108→If any file or suite is below 95% on any metric, treat it as a bug. Proactively identify and fix the lowest-coverage test suites, especially those that drag down the overall score. Do not limit yourself to only the file you were asked to test — if a neighboring suite is at 60% branches, fix it.
   109→
   110→Prioritize fixing suites with:
   111→- Low branch coverage (missed `if/else` paths, uncaught error branches)
   112→- Missing error-path tests
   113→- Large uncovered utility or service files
   114→
   115→After adding tests to a failing suite, re-run coverage to confirm the improvement before moving on.
   116→
   117→## Logger Spying
   118→
   119→Spy on Logger prototype to assert log calls without noise:
   120→
   121→```typescript
   122→loggerErrorSpy = jest.spyOn(Logger.prototype, 'error').mockImplementation(() => undefined);
   123→loggerDebugSpy = jest.spyOn(Logger.prototype, 'debug').mockImplementation(() => undefined);
   124→loggerWarnSpy = jest.spyOn(Logger.prototype, 'warn').mockImplementation(() => undefined);
   125→```
   126→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1→{
     2→	"compilerOptions": {
     3→		"module": "commonjs",
     4→		"target": "ES2023",
     5→		"declaration": true,
     6→		"removeComments": true,
     7→		"emitDecoratorMetadata": true,
     8→		"experimentalDecorators": true,
     9→		"allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
    10→		"sourceMap": true,
    11→		"outDir": "./dist",
    12→		"baseUrl": "./",
    13→		"incremental": true,
    14→		"skipLibCheck": true,
    15→		"forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    16→
    17→		// Strict Type-Checking Options
    18→		"strict": true, // Enable all strict type-checking options
    19→		"noImplicitAny": true, // Error on expressions and declarations with an implied 'any' type
    20→		"strictNullChecks": true, // Enable strict null checks
    21→		"strictFunctionTypes": true, // Enable strict checking of function types
    22→		"strictBindCallApply": true, // Enable strict 'bind', 'call', and 'apply' methods on functions
    23→		"noImplicitThis": true, // Error on 'this' expressions with an implied 'any' type
    24→		"alwaysStrict": true, // Parse in strict mode and emit "use strict" for each source file
    25→		"strictPropertyInitialization": false, // Ensure non-undefined class properties are initialized in the constructor
    26→
    27→		// Additional Checks
    28→		"noUnusedLocals": true, // Error on unused local variables
    29→		"noUnusedParameters": true, // Error on unused parameters
    30→		"noImplicitReturns": true, // Error when not all code paths in function return a value
    31→		"noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true, // Error on fallthrough cases in switch statements
    32→		"noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true, // Add 'undefined' to index signature results
    33→		"noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature": true, // Error on property access via string index when type doesn't explicitly allow it
    34→		"exactOptionalPropertyTypes": true, // Differentiate between undefined and not present
